info.title: BTC Embedded Platform - RESTful API documentation
info.description: This is the documentation for the BTC Embedded Platform RESTful API.

tags.name: Back-to-Back test reports
tags.description: Creates a Back-to-Back test report for a given Back-to-Back test. Retrieves all existing Back-to-Back reports from the profile.

tags.name: Regression Test Reports
tags.description: Creates a Regression Test Report for a given Regression Test. Retrieves all existing Regression Test reports from the profile.

tags.name: Tolerances
tags.description: Import, reset and retrieve global and local tolerances.

tags.name: dRequirement-Based Test Cases Synchronization
tags.description: Synchronize changes of Test Cases between Embedded Platform and Application Lifecycle Management Tools.

tags.name: User-defined Architecture Import
tags.description: Import a User-defined architecture from a XML file that describes the interface of the architecture.

tags.name: Coverage generation
tags.description: For a C-Code function, create stimuli vectors which cover the code function, or mark coverage properties that are unreachable.

tags.name: Code Coverage/Robustness Check B2B
tags.description: Retrieve code coverage/robustness checks details and results in B2B.

tags.name: Progress
tags.description: Get the current status of long-running operations.

tags.name: Model Coverage Reports
tags.description: Creates RBT or B2B model coverage reports.

tags.name: Formal Test Reports
tags.description: Create and get formal test reports.

tags.name: Input restrictions import/export
tags.description: Import or export input restrictions from or into a file.

tags.name: Requirements
tags.description: Retrieve Requirements, Linked Requirements, or Requirement Sources.

tags.name: Code Coverage/Robustness Check RBT
tags.description: Retrieve code coverage/robustness checks details and results in RBT.

tags.name: Real-Time Testing
tags.description: Execute operations with Real-Time Testing observers such as export

tags.name: User defined coverage goals
tags.description: Handle user defined coverage goals operations.

patch.summary: Change a verdict status for a comparison
patch.description: Changes verdict status for a comparison. If accept is true, the comparison verdict status is changed from 'failed' to 'failed (accepted)'. If accept is false, the comparison verdict status is changed from 'failed (accepted)'to 'failed'.

parameters.name: b2b-uid
parameters.description: The Back-to-Back test UID for which to change the comparison verdict.

requestBody.description: Provide the comparison and the acceptance flag.

schema.example: No Back-to-Back test was found for UID.

delete.description: Deletes the B2B test with the given ID

parameters.name: b2b-test-uid
parameters.description: The UID of the B2B test to delete

post.summary: Create a B2B test on a folder
post.description: Long running task Generates a Back-to-Back test on a given folder UID for the specified reference and comparison execution kinds. Optionally, the execution can be forced to simulate all contained requirements-based test cases/stimuli-vectors. Optionally, only stimuli vectors can be set to be executed if force execution is activated. Otherwise if there are requirements-based test cases with comparison verdicts, the verdicts will be deleted in order to run the Back-to-Back test.

parameters.name: folder-uid
parameters.description: The folder UID for which the Back-to-Back test is generated.

requestBody.description: Provides the reference and comparison execution kinds for which the Back-to-Back test will be generated. Optionally, the execution can be forced to simulate all contained test cases/stimuli-vectors. Optionally, only stimuli vectors can be set to be executed if force execution is activated. Otherwise if there are Requirements-based Test Cases with comparison verdicts, the verdicts will be deleted in order to run the Back-to-Back test.

202.description: Long running operation started. The status of the operation can be reviewed by using the Progress i.e. GET '/ep/progress?progress-id={progress-id}' using the id received by this command.

schema.example: No folder was found for the given UID.
Cannot start a new long-running operation: Previous operation is not finished, yet!

get.description: The long running task status and result will be transmitted.

201.description: Created. When the long running task is finished, the following object is availabe in the 'result' field of the response object.

get.description: Use this command to retrieve the global tolerances. A valid scope uid and use case must be specified. The lead-lag-unit can be either Seconds or Steps.

parameters.name: scope-id
parameters.description: The scope from which to retrieve the global tolerances.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: The use case kind. Can be either RBT or B2B.

parameters.name: lead-lag-unit
parameters.description: For existing tolerances, lead and lag values should be displayed as either SECONDS or STEPS. Default is STEPS.

put.description: Use this command to import the global tolerances.

requestBody.description: All necessary information for importing tolerances.

delete.description: Use this command to reset the global tolerances for the profile.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: The use case kind. Can be either RBT or B2B.

get.description: Use this command to retrieve the local tolerances. A valid test case uid must be specified. The lead-lag-unit can be either Seconds or Steps. Setting the create-if-missing to true will create local tolerances from the global tolerances.

parameters.name: test-case-id
parameters.description: The UID of the test case for which local tolerances will be retrieved.

parameters.name: lead-lag-unit
parameters.description: For existing tolerances, lead and lag values should be displayed as either SECONDS or STEPS. Default is STEPS.

parameters.name: create-if-missing
parameters.description: If true, the local tolerances will be created if they do not exist yet. Default is: false.

put.description: Import local tolerances for a given test case.

parameters.description: The UID of the test case for which local tolerances will be imported.

post.summary: Execute all test cases from a folder
post.description: Long running task Executes a requirement-based Test on all Test Cases from a given folder for the specified execution kinds. Optionally, also the model coverage report can be generated or the test execution can be forced to not re-use any previous results.

parameters.name: folder-uid
parameters.description: The folder UID for which the requirement-based Test is executed.

requestBody.description: Provide the list of execution kinds  (example: SIL, TL MIL, SL MIL, SL MIL (Toplevel), etc.) for which to execute the Test Cases. Optionally, you can choose to also generate a model coverage report for each execution kind (by default they will not be generated) or set the test execution to forced, so to not re-use any previous execution results.

202.description: Long running operation started. The status of the operation can be reviewed by using the Progress i.e. GET '/ep/progress?progress-id={progress-id}' using the id received by this command.

schema.example: Cannot start a new long-running operation: Previous operation is not finished, yet!

get.description: The long running task status and result will be transmitted.

201.description: Executed. When the long running task is finished, the following object is availabe in the 'result' field of the response object.

schema.example: No folder was found for folder UID {folder-uid}.

get.summary: Get coverage details for a scope
get.description: Get all code coverage/robustness checks details for a scope. Some filters can be applied.

parameters.name: scope-uid
parameters.description: The UID of the scope

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description:
The status filter for code coverage/robustness check goals. Possible values: COVERED, UNKNOWN, UNREACHABLE_INF, UNREACHABLE_N, INCONSISTENT, UNKNOWN_JUSTIFIED, UNREACHABLE_INF_JUSTIFIED, UNREACHABLE_N_JUSTIFIED. Can also specify multiple options.
Can also be empty, in which case the results are shown for all statuses.

parameters.name: goal-types
parameters.description:
The goal type filter for code coverage/robustness check goals. Possible values for code coverage goals: STM(Statement), C(Condition), D(Decision/Branch), CDC(C/DC), MCDC(C/DC and MC/DC), F(Function), SC(Switch-Case), RO(Relational Operator), FC(Function Call).
Possible values for robustness check goals are: DZ(Division by Zero), CA(Downcast), AOB(Array out of Bounds). Can also specify multiple options. Can also be empty, in which case the results are shown for all goal types.

parameters.name: files
parameters.description: List of file filters for code coverage/robustness check goals. If list is empty the results are shown for all files.

schema.example: The provided input data is invalid.
No scope was found for this uid.

get.summary: Get coverage details for a requirement source
get.description: Get code coverage/robustness checks details for a requirement source. Some filters can be applied.

parameters.name: requirement-source-uid
parameters.description: The UID of the requirement source

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: The status filter for code coverage/robustness check goals. Possible values: COVERED, UNKNOWN, UNREACHABLE_INF, UNREACHABLE_N, INCONSISTENT, UNKNOWN_JUSTIFIED, UNREACHABLE_INF_JUSTIFIED, UNREACHABLE_N_JUSTIFIED. Can also specify multiple options. Can also be empty, in which case the results are shown for all statuses.

parameters.name: goal-types
parameters.description: The goal type filter for code coverage/robustness check goals. Possible values for code coverage goals: STM(Statement), C(Condition), D(Decision/Branch), CDC(C/DC), MCDC(C/DC and MC/DC), F(Function), SC(Switch-Case), RO(Relational Operator), FC(Function Call). Possible values for robustness check goals are: DZ(Division by Zero), CA(Downcast), AOB(Array out of Bounds). Can also specify multiple options. Can also be empty, in which case the results are shown for all goal types.

parameters.name: files
parameters.description: List of file filters for code coverage/robustness check goals. If list is empty the results are shown for all files.

importKind.description: The kind a file should be imported as. Possible values are: "TC", "EXCEL". "TC" by default

items.description: The uids of the requirements that need to be linked to test cases.
The uids of the test cases that need to be linked to the requirements.

List of execution kinds (example: SIL, TL MIL, SL MIL, etc.). Not case-sensitive. Please note: this parameter is used for detecting if test execution results are synchronized or not.When execution kinds are specified, the corresponding test results are always synchronized, regardless of the specified syncMode. If the value is empty list or it is not specified, no test results are synchronized.

requirementsSourceUid.description: The requirement source to which to bind the test case source.

folderUid.description: The folder in which to create test cases during synchronization. If none is specified, the test cases folder defined in the test case source configuration is considered or the default test cases folder if there is no test cases source configured yet.

scopeUid.description: The scope on which to create test cases during synchronization. If none is specified, the test cases scope defined in the test case source configuration is considered or the top level scope if there is no test cases source configured yet.

draftMode.description: Specifies if draft mode shall be set on test cases during synchronization. If none is specified, the draft mode setting defined in the test case source configuration is considered or default is 'false' if there is no test cases source configured yet.

syncMode.description: EP: Synchronizes test cases (create new ones, update metadata) between ALM and EP. Resolve any conflicts with metadata from EP. ALM: Synchronizes test cases (create new ones, update metadata) between ALM and EP. Resolve any conflicts with metadata from ALM. ER_ONLY: Only Execution Results are persisted to ALM from synchronized test cases from EP. Defaults to 'ER_ONLY'.

key.description: For DOORS_NEXT, the mandatory keys are: dng_catalog_url, dng_project_id, username, pwd.The optional keys are: dng_bundle_configuration (e.g. https://ep-jazz.prom.local:9443/qm/oslc_config/resources/com.ibm.team.vvc.Configuration/_bfoUAB50Ee-hBMjyyttupw), proxy_address, proxy_port, proxy_user, proxy_password. For POLARION, the mandatory keys are: username, pwd, Host, Port, Work Item Types, Project ID, Linked Requirement Role. The optional keys are: additional_attr, Query Text.

useExistingCode.description:
Specifies if code generation needs to be done.
'true': Code generation is not explicitly performed.
Instead it is assumed that the required code files are already generated before import.
'false': Code generation is explicitly performed during the analysis.
The resulting code is used for further steps.
Default is 'false'.

parameterHandling.description:
Specifies how parameter variables are handled. Can be 'OFF' or 'EXPLICIT_PARAMETER'.
'OFF': Only regular inputs in the interface of subsystems are observed.
'EXPLICIT_PARAMETER': Parameter variables are regarded as additional
inputs to subsystems.
Default is 'EXPLICIT_PARAMETER'.
If not specified (undefined or invalid), the default value is used.

testMode.description:
Specifies the test mode. Can be 'BLACK_BOX' or 'GREY_BOX'.
If set to 'GreyBox', local variables are regarded as additional outputs of subsystems.
For BlackBox-Testing only the regularoutputs in the interfaces of subsystems are observed.
Default is 'GreyBox'.
If not specified (undefined or invalid), the default value is used.

fixedStepSolver.description:
Handling when a non-fixed-step solver is ecountered: If 'true', the solver is automatically set to fixed-step.
Otherwise an error is issued.Default is 'false'.

inDepthCcodeAnalysis.description:
true: Simulink model and C-Code model are imported.
false: indicates that a SL SIL simulation is supported.
C-Code and mapping are omitted.
Default is 'true'

subsystemMatcher.description:
A whitelist of all subsystems you would like to import.
Uses the regular expression standard.
Each subsystem is identified by its virtual path inside the model.
If no value is defined, all subsystems are imported.
If the specified regular expression does not produce any match, an error is reported.

parameterMatcher.description:
A whitelist of all parameters you would like to import.
Uses the regular expression standard.
Each parameter is identified by its name.
If no value is defined, all calibrations are imported.
If the specified regular expression does not produce any match, no calibration is imported.
The list cannot be applied, if parameterHandling is set to 'OFF'.
Please note, that model workspace parameters have their name extended by the model they
are located in and need to be adressed accordingly in the following way: 'modelname:paramname'

constantsMatcher.description:
A whitelist of all constants you would like to import.
Uses the regular expression standard.
Each constant is identified by its name.
If no value is defined, no constants are imported.
If the specified regular expression does not produce any match, no constant is imported.

cfileMatcher.description:
A whitelist of all c-code files you would like to import.
Uses the regular expression standard.
Each file is identified by its name.
If no value is defined, all files are imported.
If the specified regular expression does not produce any match, no file is imported.

tlModelFile.description: The absolute or relativ path to the TargetLink model file (.mdl|.slx).

tlInitScript.description: The absolute or relativ path to the script defining all parameters needed for initializing the TL-model. If not provided, the TL-model is assumed to be selfcontained.

fixedStepSolver.description:
'true': The analyzed models (TargetLink model and Simulink model) will be set to the fixed-step solver type automatically. The usage of the EmbeddedPlatform requires a fixed-step solver. If the model is open and the fixed-step solver is not already set, this might lead to a modified model.
'false': The analyzed models (TargetLink model and Simulink model) will not be set to the fixed-step solver automatically. If the fixed-step solver is not already set in the model, an exception is thrown. In order to proceed the user has to set the fixed-step solver manually in the simulation settings.
Note: The option is ignored if the model is currently not in an open/loaded state. In this case it has no visible side-effect.
Default is 'false'.

tlSubsystem.description:
Name of the Subsystem representing the TL toplevel subsystem for the analysis.
Note: Argument is obligatory if there is more than one toplevel system in the model.

calibrationHandling.description:
Determine how calibration variables are being handled.
'OFF': Only regular inputs in the interface of subsystems are observed.
'EXPLICIT_PARAMETER': Calibration variables are regarded as additional inputs to subsystems. Their value is set once during the initial phase of the simulation and is held constant thereafter.
'LIMITED_BLOCKSET': Calibration variables are regarded as additional inputs to subsystems. Their value is set once during the initial phase of the simulation and is held constant thereafter. Enable support for calibration within block properties, not using workspace calibrations.
Note: The supported Set of calibratable TL-Blocks is different from 'EXPLICIT_PARAMETER'.
Default is 'EXPLICIT_PARAMETER'.

subsystemMatcher.description:
A whitelist of all subsystems you would like to import.
Uses the regular expression standard.
Each subsystem is identified by its virtual path inside the model.
If no value is defined, all subsystems are imported.
If the specified regular expression does not produce any match, only the toplevel subsystem is imported.
The root subsystem is always imported.
Please note that the subsystem-path has to include the Targetlink wrapper.

calibrationMatcher.description:
A whitelist of all calibrations you would like to import.
Uses the regular expression standard.
Each calibration is identified by its name.
If no value is defined, all calibrations are imported.
If the specified regular expression does not produce any match, no calibration is imported.
The list cannot be applied, if calibrationHandling is set to 'OFF'.

constantsMatcher.description:
A whitelist of all constants you would like to import.
Uses the regular expression standard.
Each constant is identified by its name.
If no value is defined, no constants are imported.
If the specified regular expression does not produce any match, no constant is imported.

sdfFile.description: The absolute path to the SDF file. This file is mandatory and may not be null! If the specified file path is invalid, an error is reported.

trcFile.description: The absolute path to the TRC file. This file is mandatory and may not be null! If the specified file path is invalid, an error is reported.

sampleTime.description: The SDF architecture sample time in seconds. The format can be only numbers (e.g. 3) or with the time unit specified (e.g. 3 s). Attempt to use any other format will be reported as an error.

items.description: Subsystems names that should not be imported, can be empty. If an invalid subsystem is requested, an error is reported.

propagateSelection.description: This option allows to configure the excluded subsystems option. If set to true, also the child subsystems of the excluded subsystems are excluded from the import. Default setting is false.

The absolute or relative path to the script defining all parameters needed for initializing the model.
If not provided, the model is assumed to be selfcontained.

useExistingCode.description:
Determine if code generation needs to be done.
'true': Code generation is not explicitly performed.
Instead it is assumed that the required code files are already generated and
that the corresponding DataDictionary of the model includes information about the code generation.
'false': Code generation is explicitly performed during the analysis.
The resulting code is used for further steps.

Default is 'false'.

calibrationHandling.description:
Determine how calibration variables are being handled.
'OFF': Only regular inputs in the interface of subsystems are observed.
'EXPLICIT_PARAMETER': Calibration variables are regarded as additional inputs to subsystems.
Their value is set once during the initial phase of the simulation and is held constant thereafter.
'LIMITED_BLOCKSET': Calibration variables are regarded as additional inputs to subsystems.
Their value is set once during the initial phase of the simulation and is held constant thereafter.
Enable support for calibration within block properties, not using workspace calibrations.
Note: 'LIMITED_BLOCKSET' is only valid for conversion to a TargetLink architecture.
Note: The supported Set of calibratable TL-Blocks is different from 'EXPLICIT_PARAMETER'.

Default is 'EXPLICIT_PARAMETER'.

testMode.description:
Specifies the test mode. Can be 'BLACK_BOX' or 'GREY_BOX'.
If set to 'GREY_BOX', local variables are regarded as additional outputs of subsystems.
For Black Box-Testing only the regular outputs in the interfaces of subsystems are observed.

Default is 'GREY_BOX'.

fixedStepSolver.description:
Defines, if the fixed step solver will be set or not.
'true': The analyzed models (TargetLink model and Simulink model) will be set to the fixed-step solver type automatically.
The usage of the EmbeddedPlatform requires a fixed-step solver.
If the model is open and the fixed-step solver is not already set,
this might lead to a modified model.
'false': The analyzed models (TargetLink model and Simulink model) will not be set to the fixed-step solver automatically.
If the fixed-step solver is not already set in the model, an exception is thrown.
In order to proceed the user has to set the fixed-step solver manually in the simulation settings.
Note: The option is ignored if the model is currently not in an open/loaded state.
In this case it has no visible side-effect.

Default is 'false'.

environment.description:
The absolute or relative path to XML file including information about environmental files like additional code for TargetLink.
See specifications in CodeGeneration.dtd.
Note: The option is only valid for a conversion to TargetLink.

activateModelLinking.description:
Determine if the source code needs to be linked to the TargetLink model.
'true': A link between the source code and the TargetLink model will be established.
This setting may lead to a modified TargetLink model.
To accomplish this link relation, the TargetLink option 'ExtendedBlockComments' needs to be enabled
with a subsequent TargetLink code generation.
'false': The source code model linking is not explictitly set by EmbeddedPlatform.
Note: The option is only valid for a conversion to TargetLink.

Default is 'false'.

closedLoopModel.description:
Determine if the TargetLink SUT environment is analyzed during analysis.
'true': The environment of the SUT is also analyzed during the model extraction.
Important for analyzing closed-loop models.
'false': Only the SUT is considered during the model extraction.
Note: The option is only valid for a conversion to TargetLink.

Default is 'false'.

Default is 'false'.

mappingFileForTLArch.description:
The absolute or relative path to the mapping file for TargetLink architecture mapping.
At least one mapping file must be provided if architectures are already available in the profile. The file is used to map the first imported architecture to the new imported TargetLink architecture.
If this file is not provided, the mapping to the first imported architecture is derived from the auto-generated
mapping between TargetLink, C-Code, and Simulink (if available). For the derivation to work, at least one of
these architectures must be mapped to the first imported architecture via a specified mapping file

mappingFileForCCodeArch.description:
The absolute or relative path to the mapping file for C-Code architecture mapping.
At least one mapping must be provided if architectures are already available in the profile. The file is used to map the first imported architecture to the new imported C-Code architecture.
If this file is not provided, the mapping to the first imported architecture is derived from the auto-generated
mapping between TargetLink, C-Code, and Simulink (if available). For the derivation to work, at least one of
these architectures must be mapped to the first imported architecture via a specified mapping file

mappingFileForSLArch.description:
The absolute or relative path to the mapping file for Simulink architecture mapping.
At least one mapping must be provided if a Simulink architecture is additionally imported and architectures are already available in the profile.
The file is used to map the first imported architecture to the new imported Simulink architecture.
If this file is not provided, the mapping to the first imported architecture is derived from the auto-generated
mapping between TargetLink, C-Code, and Simulink (if available). For the derivation to work, at least one of
these architectures must be mapped to the first imported architecture via a specified mapping file

items.description: The formal requirements for which the coverage will be generated.

behaviour.description:
Specifies if the vector generation shall be based on the behaviour of the SUT or the behaviour specified via formal requirements.
 'IMPLEMENTATION' (default, if profile contains SUT): Uses behaviour from SUT
'FORMAL_REQUIREMENTS': Uses behaviour described in formal requirements (see SurroundingFormalRequirements).

architecture.description:
Architecture uid for which generation is performed. 
 (Only valid for behaviour: FORMAL_REQUIREMENTS).

items.description:
Specifies the formal requirements that describe the behaviour as array. (only valid for corresponding 'Behaviour' selection)
By default all formal requirements of the scope (see first parameter) are taken into account.

restrictOutputBehaviour.description:
Set whether or not values for outputs and locals should keep their values if these are not written by formal requirement
(only valid for Behaviour: Formal Requirements). 
Default is 'true'

coverageMetric.description:
Specifies the type of coverage metric.
Default is 'ONCE'

coverageTarget.description:
Specifies the type of coverage target. Allowed values depend on the selected 'coverageMetric':
'NONE' (default) for 'ONCE' coverageMetric,
'TRIGGER_EVENT_COVERAGE' (default), 'TRIGGER_CONDITION_COVERAGE', or 'TRIGGER_EVENT_CONDITION_COVERAGE' (Trigger event/condition coverage) for'LINEAR_CONDITION_COVERAGE' or 'MULTIPLE_CONDITION_COVERAGE' coverageMetric.
